articles.title: China blocks Meta from acquiring AI startup Manus
articles.summary: Meta said Monday that the transaction "complied fully with applicable law" and that it anticipates "an appropriate resolution to the inquiry."

articles.title: The Small U.K. Agency That’s a 911 for Ships in the Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: At a military base outside Portsmouth, England, a 24-hour service monitors the Persian Gulf, the Red Sea and part of the Indian Ocean, responding to distress calls.

articles.title: King Charles Visits Trump as U.S.-U.K. Tensions Flare, and Airlines Ask White House for Help
articles.summary: Plus, how millions of people could become Canadian.

articles.title: The MAHA movement is mad about glyphosate and Trump's EPA
articles.summary: Some people in the MAHA movement are angry with the Trump administration's stance on environmental toxins — including its current support for the maker of the pesticide glyphosate.

articles.title: 'We don't know what will happen to us': U.S. deportees in limbo in DRC
articles.summary: 15 South American migrants and asylum seekers deported from the U.S. to the DRC are now living in uncertainty in a country an with ongoing armed conflict, where they have no ties.

articles.title: Millions of homes in the U.S. are uninsured. NPR wants to hear your story
articles.summary: Millions of home in the U.S. are uninsured, partly because insurance costs have soared in recent years. NPR wants to hear about the coverage decisions you're making as premiums rise.

articles.title: Lawsuits accuse State Farm of secretly working to cut insurance payouts
articles.summary: Lawsuits allege that State Farm tries to avoid paying what it owes for hail damage. The litigation is happening as homeowners face soaring insurance costs, partly due to threats from climate change.

articles.title: Transition to a New Fed Chair Is Unlikely to Mean Immediate Rate Cuts
articles.summary: The Federal Reserve is expected to hold interest rates steady this week as Jerome H. Powell presides over what is likely to be his last meeting as chair.

articles.title: Republicans Brace for Brutal Midterms as Trump’s Popularity Slips
articles.summary: The elections are still six months off, and some within the G.O.P. say there is still time to right the ship.

articles.title: Graduates Reset Ambitions in Pursuit of First Jobs
articles.summary: Young people aiming to build careers are entering fields they had not considered to find their footing.

articles.title: Oil Prices Jump Again as U.S.-Iran Talks Appear Deadlocked
articles.summary: Investors parsed reports that President Trump told advisers that he was not satisfied with Iran’s latest proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Should schools get rid of homework? Some educators are saying yes
articles.summary: Some experts worry that less homework could be a problem for math achievement, at a time when test scores nationwide are already at a dismal low.

articles.title: Stuck in limbo: millions of professionals risk losing legal status under Trump pause
articles.summary: Their experiences — of sudden financial insecurity, months of unemployment, and crippling anxiety — come as the administration seeks to restrict legal migration and boost mass deportation.

articles.title: So far, Florida has failed to end vaccine mandates. Now there's a last-ditch effort
articles.summary: Florida Republicans have pushed hard to drop some vaccine requirements for children. But after many months, their efforts stalled out. Could this week's special legislative session get the job done?

articles.title: Trump's embrace of King Charles comes at a fraught time for U.S.-UK relations
articles.summary: Trump seems to be looking forward to hosting, in recent weeks bringing up the royal visit multiple times.

articles.title: Violence escalates in Colombia with dozens of attacks before presidential vote
articles.summary: A spate of attacks against civilians and military bases in Colombia's southwestern region has raised security concerns as the country heads to a May presidential election.

articles.title: Pompeii archaeologists use AI to reconstruct man killed in volcano's eruption
articles.summary: Archaeologists have used AI for the first time to digitally reconstruct the face of a man killed in the AD 79 eruption of Mount Vesuvius, offering a new way to understand one of history's most famous

articles.title: Trump Is Dissatisfied With Iran’s Plan to Reopen Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: The proposal would have set aside questions about what to do with Iran’s nuclear program.

articles.title: C.E.O.s Lean In on ‘Resilience’ to Manage Global Turmoil
articles.summary: The ability to stay calm and lead through any kind of shock is the new normal for corporate executives.

articles.title: Alleged Correspondents' Dinner shooter to appear in court. And, Charles III visits U.S.
articles.summary: The suspect in the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting incident is set to appear in federal court today. And, King Charles III and Queen Camilla arrive in Washington today for a state visit.

articles.title: Iran's flurry of diplomacy continues in Russia, as Trump reviews Iran's latest proposal
articles.summary: Iran's foreign minister arrived in Russia on Monday, after a whirlwind weekend of diplomacy, seeking to gain political leverage and foreign backing as peace talks with the U.S. remain on hold.

articles.title: East Africa redefines marathon limits as Sabastian Sawe leads historic charge
articles.summary: East Africa has rewritten marathon history as Sabastian Sawe produced a stunning breakthrough at the London Marathon, redefining what was thought possible over the marathon distance.

articles.title: Palestinian officials hail local elections in a Gaza community and the West Bank
articles.summary: Palestinian officials say local elections in Gaza and the West Bank mark a step toward a long-delayed presidential election. The Palestinian Authority hasn't held a presidential election in 21 years.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
